_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Utilize tools and techniques like risk assessment, threat modeling, and cybersecurity to detect and analyze the threats.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting AI security assessments, including threat modeling for AI systems, attack surface analysis, and control gap identification.
comments: DCWF task superset of WIDAI RM domain. RM-S-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RM-T-002 (RM, cosine: 0.549), SP-S-003 (SP, cosine: 0.512).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.6/10 (raw 0.763). Shared vocabulary: [modeling, threat]. DCWF-only terms [analyze, assessment, cybersecurity, detect, like, risk] vs WIDAI-only terms [ai, analysis, assessments, attack, conducting, control] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.900 (P=0.908, R=0.892). Balanced P/R — the 17-word DCWF element and 19-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.2%). "Utilize tools and techniques like risk assessment, threat modeling, and cybersec" and "Skill in conducting AI security assessments, including threat modeling for AI sy" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.554 < STS 0.763 (gap -0.209). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [analyze, assessment, cybersecurity] and [ai, analysis, assessments] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 10.0%. Shared tokens: [modeling, threat]. DCWF-only: [analyze, assessment, cybersecurity, detect, like, risk]. WIDAI-only: [ai, analysis, assessments, attack, conducting, control].
